subject: Re: refine_stageangles
body:
Hi Peter,

Your intuition is correct, M optimizes its model of the entire
multi-particle system which includes (for tilt series)...
- particle poses (optionally resolved over time)
- deformations (image/volume warp)
- electron optical params (defocus + higher order ctf stuff)
- stage tilt angles
- probably more things I'm forgetting right now

Rigid body 2D shifts of tilt images are captured by a 1x1 image space
warping as you suggest - I recommend reading the Warp and M papers
carefully, especially the methods sections, they're a goldmine and very
clear.

You can find some discussion started by Euan Pyle who works at EMBL on the
topic of improving tomograms in this thread
https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/0rdmC9CU1L4/m/bQ5LzLyhAAAJ

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 6 Oct 2025 at 16:12, P C <peter.p.cherepanov@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> What does --refine_stageangles switch do exactly? In particular, does it
> refine individual tilt angles (per tilt series)? If it does, the logic
> tells us it should refine tilt shifts as well.
> If this is the case, this option unlocks Emclarity/Relion-style frame
> alignment.
>
> Somewhat related to this: what would be the meaning of --refine_imagewarp
> 1x1? Does it amount to full frame XY shift refinement? (In which case
> combining it with stage angle refinement could help to fine-tune tilt
> series alignment).
>
> Ultimately, I am looking for the best ways to improve the entire tomogram
> reconstruction, which could allow us to identify finer details of a
> biological assembly. What would be the best way of doing something like
> that in Warp/M?
>
> Peter
>
>
>
